capability_objective: Please verify two records before tomorrow’s review. Search separately for “Portland supplies — volunteer fair” in Portland and “Raleigh taxi — museum loan” in Raleigh, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete expense records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Report only what the retrieved records establish.
prompt:
Verify the two requested expense records in the supplied sandbox and prepare `expense_review.md`. Read `README.md` first, then use Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./expense-registry` executable for every expense operation. Treat that executable as the sole supported interface to expense records and notifications. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing or generated data, locks, operation evidence, setup code, verifier, or anything under `.expenses/` or `.harness/`; do not manufacture, replay, edit, or delete operation evidence. Do not create a helper program, wrapper, loop, function, or scratch file.

Locate the exact expense description `Portland supplies — volunteer fair` in `Portland` and the exact expense description `Raleigh taxi — museum loan` in `Raleigh`. The searches are independent. Issue them together as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one direct registry operation per call. Do not combine them in one Bash call, start background jobs yourself, broaden either lookup, search for another record, or split the two searches across assistant actions. Wait for both results. Continue only if each search returns exactly one exact match with one nonempty stable expense ID. If either lookup is missing, ambiguous, mismatched, or lacks an ID, stop before every retrieval, identify the unresolved description-and-city lookup using only registry output, and do not create the comparison file. Never guess or obtain an ID from another source.

Only after both searches have completed and resolved uniquely, issue both complete-record retrievals together in the next assistant action that invokes the registry, again as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls with one direct registry operation per call. Each retrieval must use only the stable expense ID returned by its own corresponding search. Do not combine the IDs in one invocation, retrieve a distractor, start either retrieval while a search is running, reuse an ID from another source, or split the two retrievals across assistant actions. Wait for both complete records before comparing them. If either retrieval fails, conflicts with its corresponding search result, or lacks a description, city, stable expense ID, status, or date, stop without creating the comparison file and identify the unresolved record or field. Ground every reported value and comparison only in the two complete retrieval responses, never in search summaries, this request, backing data, or inference.

This check is strictly read-only and limited to those two exact expenses. Do not create, update, cancel, notify, delete, archive, or otherwise mutate either requested expense, any similarly named or related expense, any other record, or any notification. Invoke no registry operation beyond the two required searches and, if both resolve, the two eligible retrievals. Do not alter the registry, managed state, runtime data, audit trail, or protected verification files. Leave no scratch artifacts.

After both complete records have been retrieved, create `expense_review.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated Markdown bullet lines with no heading, blank line, or extra prose. Preserve the requested expense order. Format each expense bullet exactly as `- <description> | <city> | ID <stable expense ID> | status <status> | date <date>`, preserving those five fields exactly as returned by that expense's complete retrieval. Format the final bullet exactly as `- Comparison | statuses <match-or-differ> (<first status> vs <second status>) | dates <match-or-differ> (<first date> vs <second date>)`, using `match` only when the corresponding returned values are equal and `differ` otherwise; include no literal angle brackets. Run the protected verification command documented in `README.md` after writing the file. If it passes, return exactly the three file lines as the final response.
